title: Car Storage Costs: Complete Pricing Guide for 2026
excerpt: How much does car storage cost? A comprehensive breakdown of pricing by storage type, location, and vehicle class, plus tips to reduce your monthly bill without sacrificing protection.
content:
How Much Does Car Storage Actually Cost?

Car storage costs range from $45 per month for a basic outdoor lot to $3,000 or more per month for full-service concierge storage. The price you pay depends on where you live, what you drive, and how much protection you need.

This guide breaks down 2026 pricing for every storage type, explains the factors that push costs up or down, and shares practical strategies to save money without compromising on the care your vehicle deserves.

Average Monthly Costs by Storage Type

Here is what car owners across the United States are paying in 2026, organized by storage category.

Outdoor Storage

  • Open lot with perimeter fencing: $45 to $100 per month
  • Covered outdoor with carport or canopy: $75 to $150 per month

Outdoor storage is the most budget-friendly option. It works well for daily drivers, trucks, boats, and RVs that are built to handle the elements. However, prolonged sun exposure fades paint and degrades rubber seals, so this is not ideal for vehicles you want to keep in showroom condition.

Indoor Storage

  • Shared warehouse or garage bay: $100 to $250 per month
  • Individual enclosed unit: $150 to $400 per month

Indoor storage eliminates weather and UV exposure. It is the most popular option for seasonal vehicles, classic cars, and anything you want shielded from rain, snow, and hail. Most indoor facilities include basic security such as gated access and surveillance cameras.

Climate-Controlled Storage

  • Standard climate-controlled: $150 to $500 per month
  • Premium climate-controlled with humidity regulation: $300 to $800 per month

Climate control adds temperature and humidity management on top of indoor protection. This is essential for high-value vehicles, convertibles with soft tops, and any car stored for more than three months. Stable conditions prevent condensation, mold, rubber degradation, and electronic corrosion. Learn more about climate-controlled car storage.

Luxury and Concierge Storage

  • Luxury storage with security and climate control: $500 to $1,500 per month
  • Full concierge service for exotics and collectors: $1,000 to $3,000+ per month

Concierge storage is the top tier. It includes everything from battery tending and periodic engine starts to full detailing, enclosed transport, and airport vehicle delivery. Facilities at this level typically store only high-value vehicles and maintain strict access controls. Explore exotic car storage options.

What Drives Car Storage Pricing?

Understanding the factors behind pricing helps you find the best value for your situation.

Location

Geography is the largest single factor. A basic indoor spot in Manhattan can cost more than premium climate-controlled storage in a smaller Midwestern city. Facilities just outside major metro areas often deliver the same quality at 30 to 50 percent lower prices. If you are flexible on location, storing 30 to 60 minutes outside a city center can save hundreds per month.

Vehicle Size

Standard pricing assumes a sedan or coupe. SUVs, trucks, and lifted vehicles typically require oversized spots that cost 15 to 30 percent more. RVs, boats, and trailers have entirely separate rate structures, often priced by linear foot.

Contract Length

Most facilities reward longer commitments. Month-to-month rates are full price, while six-month contracts typically save 10 to 15 percent and annual contracts save 15 to 25 percent. If you know your timeline, locking in a longer term is one of the easiest ways to reduce your bill.

Amenities and Add-On Services

Each added service increases the monthly cost. Common extras include battery tending at $15 to $30 per month, periodic engine starts at $25 to $50 per month, detailing services ranging from $100 to $500 per session, and enclosed vehicle transport at $1.50 to $3.00 per mile. Evaluate which services you genuinely need versus which you can handle yourself during monthly visits.

Seasonal Demand

Storage follows predictable demand cycles. In northern states, prices rise in late fall as owners put convertibles and classics away for winter. In warm-weather states, summer storage for secondary vehicles can create price spikes. Booking before peak season often secures a better rate.

Car Storage Costs by Major City

Typical monthly indoor storage rates for 2026 across major US metros:

  • New York City: $300 to $900
  • Los Angeles: $200 to $600
  • Chicago: $150 to $450
  • Houston: $100 to $350
  • Miami: $200 to $550
  • San Francisco: $300 to $800
  • Dallas: $100 to $350
  • Seattle: $150 to $450
  • Phoenix: $100 to $300
  • Denver: $125 to $375
  • Atlanta: $100 to $350
  • Boston: $250 to $650

Search AutoVault to see current pricing from facilities in your area.

Seven Ways to Save on Car Storage

1. Compare at Least Three Facilities

Prices can vary 50 percent or more between facilities in the same area. AutoVault makes side-by-side comparison easy so you can spot the best deal quickly.

2. Commit to a Longer Term

A six- or twelve-month contract typically saves 10 to 25 percent compared to month-to-month billing. Ask facilities about their long-term rates before signing anything.

3. Look Just Outside the City

Suburban facilities 20 to 40 minutes from downtown often charge significantly less for equivalent quality. The drive is worth the savings, especially for vehicles you access infrequently.

4. Skip Services You Can Handle Yourself

If the facility charges extra for battery tending, tire pressure checks, or engine starts, consider visiting monthly to do these yourself. This is especially effective for long-term storage.

5. Adjust Your Insurance

Contact your insurer about switching to a comprehensive-only policy during storage. Dropping collision and liability coverage while the car is not on the road can save $50 to $200 per month on premiums.

6. Share a Space

Some facilities allow two smaller vehicles to share a larger bay at a reduced per-car rate. If you and a friend both need storage, this can cut individual costs substantially.

7. Ask About Off-Season Pricing

Facilities with seasonal demand fluctuations may offer discounted rates during their quieter months. It never hurts to ask.

Is Car Storage Worth the Investment?

A useful rule of thumb: if your vehicle is worth more than 100 times the monthly storage cost, the protection is a sound investment. A $50,000 car stored at $200 per month is well-protected at a fraction of its value. Compare that to the cost of repairing hail damage, repainting a sun-faded exterior, or replacing wiring chewed by rodents.

For collector and exotic vehicles, proper storage also preserves resale value. Documented storage history at a reputable facility can be a selling point that adds thousands to the eventual sale price.

Find the Right Storage at the Right Price

Car storage is not one-size-fits-all. Define the level of protection your vehicle needs, compare options in your area, and negotiate based on your commitment length. The right facility balances cost and care in a way that makes sense for your vehicle and your budget.
